revision.text: #redirect[[en:Katsushika_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社| 名称=葛飾神社| 所在地=千葉県船橋市西船5丁目3番8号| 創建=[[大正]]5年1月13日（1916年）| 社格=村社| 祭神=[[瓊瓊杵尊]]・[[彦火火出見尊]]・[[熊野大神]]| 本殿=| 例祭=| 画像=}}
'''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。
== 歴史 == 「[[葛飾]]」は[[葛]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。[[和名類聚抄]]には[[葛飾郡]]栗原郷が記され<ref>栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。</ref>、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「熊野神社」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。一方、「葛飾神社元宮」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]（ほこら）が[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の約100 [[メートル|m]]の印内公園にある。葛飾神社元宮は[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）熊野神社へ[[合祀]]された後、[[村社]]「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]している。葛飾神社の[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか)」は「一[[郡]][[惣社|総社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」」と刻まれ、葛飾神社元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]であり、葛飾神社はこれを引き継いでる。
== 境内 == 境内は広くはないが「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>。
== 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしゃ}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:船橋市]]
{{shinto-stub}}

== 歴史 == [[葛飾]]とは[[葛]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。[[和名類聚抄]]には[[葛飾郡]]栗原郷が記され<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。</ref>、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。
一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]（ほこら）が[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の約100[[メートル|m]]の印内公園にあり、葛飾神社元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅の井（[[カズラ]]のい）も近くにある。葛飾神社元宮は[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）熊野神社へ[[合祀]]され、その時に[[村社]]「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]している。葛飾神社の[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか)」は「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、葛飾神社元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の一[[郡]]の[[総社]]であり、葛飾神社はこれを引き継いでる。
== 境内 == 境内は広くはないが「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>。
== 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしゃ}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:船橋市]]
{{shinto-stub}}

== 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが葛飾とは[[葛]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。[[和名類聚抄]]には[[葛飾郡]]栗原郷が記され<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。</ref>、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。
一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]（ほこら）が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]・印内公園に並んであり、葛飾神社元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅の井（[[カズラ]]のい）も近くにある。葛飾神社元宮は[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）熊野神社へ[[合祀]]され、その時に[[村社]]「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]している。葛飾神社の[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間の再建の[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか)」は「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、葛飾神社元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の一[[郡]]の[[総社]]であり、葛飾神社はこれを引き継いでる。
== 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>。
== 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしゃ}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:船橋市]]
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社| 名称=葛飾神社| 画像=[[File:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|葛飾神社]]| 所在地=千葉県船橋市西船5丁目3番8号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[瓊瓊杵尊]]・[[彦火火出見尊]]・[[熊野大神]]| 社格=[[村社|旧村社]]| 創建=不詳| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月14日]]-15日| }} [[File:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。
== 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]]栗原郷が記され<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。</ref>、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。
一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（印内公園）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅（かずら）の井」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804-18年）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。
== 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>。
== 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしゃ}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:船橋市]]
{{shinto-stub}}

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社| 名称=葛飾神社| 画像=[[File:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|葛飾神社]]| 所在地=[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]西船五丁目3番8号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[瓊瓊杵尊]]・[[彦火火出見尊]]・[[熊野大神]]| 社格=[[村社|旧村社]]| 創建=不詳| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月14日]]-15日| }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[File:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。
== 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。</ref>、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。
一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（印内公園）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅（かずら）の井」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間（1804-18年）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。
== 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>。
== 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/麒麟檸檬|麒麟檸檬]] ([[User talk:麒麟檸檬|会話]]) による ID:44814732 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]
revision.text:
{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（印内公園）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅（かずら）の井」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]

鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（印内公園）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「葛羅（かずら）の井」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:船橋市の建築物]] [[Category:船橋市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社]]

鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} * [http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm 葛飾神社（葛飾明神）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:船橋市の建築物]] [[Category:船橋市の歴史]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社]]

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{mapplot|139.956583|35.711021|葛飾神社}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - [https://tesshow.jp/index.html 猫の足あと] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.711021|139.956583}}|caption=葛飾神社|width=256}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた祠（2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - [https://tesshow.jp/index.html 猫の足あと] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市西船五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|scale:10000}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.711021|139.956583}}|caption=葛飾神社|width=256}} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた[[合祀]]以前の[[祠]] 2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - [https://tesshow.jp/index.html 猫の足あと] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - revision.text:
{{統合文字|葛}} {{神社 |名称 = 葛飾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = 千葉県船橋市[[西船 (船橋市)|西船]]五丁目3番8号 |位置 = {{Coord|35|42|39|N|139|57|25|E|region:JP-12_scale:5000|display=it}} |祭神 = 瓊瓊杵尊・彦火火出見尊・熊野大神 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月14日-15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba#Japan Kanto|float=center|width=200|caption=|label=葛飾神社}} }} [[ファイル:Katsushika Jinja origin Funabashi Chiba.jpg|thumb|150px|「奉勧請葛飾大明神」と刻まれる石碑を納めた[[合祀]]以前の[[祠]] 2009年4月19日撮影）]] '''葛飾神社'''（かつしかじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[船橋市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == [[瓊瓊杵尊]]、[[彦火火出見尊]]、[[熊野大神]]を祭神とする。 == 歴史 == [[葛飾]]と付く地名は多いが、「葛飾」とは[[ツル植物|葛（カズラ）]]が繁り、それに飾られた豊かで美しい土地を意味する。『[[和名類聚抄]]』には[[葛飾郡]][[栗原藩|栗原郷]]が記され{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=小栗原の沿革（抜粋）|publisher=[[船橋市]]|accessdate=2009-04-13}}{{リンク切れ|date=2024年7月}}[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ 船橋市/船橋市デジタルミュージアム トップ] → ふるさと船橋の地名を知る → [https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/text-list/d100050/ht400130 小栗原]栗原郷は今は[[船橋市立小栗原小学校]]として地名を残している。、[[熊野大神]]を祀る「'''熊野神社'''」が勝間田の池（現：勝間田公園）傍の西側の丘にあった。 一方、「'''葛飾神社元宮'''」（または「葛飾大明神」・「葛飾明神」）の[[祠]]が元の位置の近傍とされる[[船橋市立葛飾中学校]]北側の[[児童公園]]（[[印内公園]]）に並んであり、元宮の[[御手洗]]の[[井戸]]と呼ばれた「[[葛羅の井|葛羅（かずら）の井]]」も近くにある。[[文化 (元号)|文化年間]]（[[1804年]] - [[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - [https://tesshow.jp/index.html 猫の足あと] {{commonscat|Katsushika-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつしかしんしや}} [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[1818年]]）に再建された[[鳥居]]の「額束（がくづか）」には「一郡[[総社|惣社]] 葛飾宮 新田末裔徳純慎[[書]]」と刻まれ、この元宮は[[葛飾郡]]の[[総社]]と崇められたようで、現在の当神社はこれを引き継いだものである。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、元宮を熊野神社へ[[合祀]]して現社名「葛飾神社」と[[改称]]、その時に[[村社]]に列した。 == 境内 == 階段上の境内は24[[坪]]と広くはないが周りに「[[船橋市|船橋]]名木10選」の[[クロマツ]]が数本ある。[[国道14号]]に面し、[[成田街道]]と呼ばれた時代から[[成田山新勝寺]]への参拝や[[佐倉街道]]へと往来する人から白眉（はくび）の松の一つとして親しまれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=葛飾神社のクロマツ|publisher=船橋市緑の基金|accessdate=2009-04-12}}。 == 交通 == 最寄駅は[[東日本旅客鉄道]]、[[東京地下鉄]]、[[東葉高速鉄道]]の[[西船橋駅]]または[[京成電鉄]]の[[京成西船駅]]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[葛飾郡]] * [[下総国]] * [[葛飾区]] * [[葛飾町]] * [[葛飾川]] * [[手児奈]] == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/05170517/funabashi/e_katsushikajinja.htm |title=葛飾神社（葛飾明神） |date=20030901033109}} *[https://tesshow.jp/chiba/funabashi/shrine_nishifna_katsushika.html 葛飾神社] - {{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
| image= File:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG
| prefecture= {{ill|Funabashi|qid=Q271417}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|35.710833|N|139.956944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

== References ==  
<references />

== See Also ==

* [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]]  
* [[Katsushika City]]  
* [[Katsushika Town]]  
* [[Katsushika River]]  
* [[Tekona]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]

}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Funabashi|Funabashi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable [[:en:black pine|black pine]] (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]], [[:en:Tokyo Metro|Tokyo Metro]], or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Nishi-Funabashi Station|Nishi-Funabashi Station]]  
* [[:en:Keisei Electric Railway|Keisei Electric Railway]] [[:en:Keisei-Nishifuna Station|Keisei-Nishifuna Station]]

{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
| image= File:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG
| prefecture= {{ill|Funabashi|WD=Q271417}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|35.710833|N|139.956944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|lt=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable {{ill|black pine|en|black pine|WD=Q4921461|lt=black pine}} (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro}}, or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Funabashi Station|en|Nishi-Funabashi Station|WD=Q128803|lt=Nishi-Funabashi Station}}  
* {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|WD=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|en|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|WD=Q6383831|lt=Keisei-Nishifuna Station}}

{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]

}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|lt=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable {{ill|black pine|en|black pine|WD=Q4921461|lt=black pine}} (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro}}, or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Funabashi Station|en|Nishi-Funabashi Station|WD=Q128803|lt=Nishi-Funabashi Station}}  
* {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|WD=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|en|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|WD=Q6383831|lt=Keisei-Nishifuna Station}}

[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|lt=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable {{ill|black pine|en|black pine|WD=Q4921461|lt=black pine}} (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro}}, or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Funabashi Station|en|Nishi-Funabashi Station|WD=Q128803|lt=Nishi-Funabashi Station}}  
* {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|WD=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|en|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|WD=Q6383831|lt=Keisei-Nishifuna Station}}

{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
| image= File:Katsushika Jinja Shrine Funabashi Chiba.JPG
| prefecture= {{ill|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|35.710833|N|139.956944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|lt=Funabashi|1=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable {{ill|black pine|en|black pine|WD=Q4921461|lt=black pine|1=Black pine}} (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro|1=Tokyo Metro}}, or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Funabashi Station|en|Nishi-Funabashi Station|WD=Q128803|lt=Nishi-Funabashi Station|1=Nishi-Funabashi Station}}  
* {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|WD=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway|1=Keisei Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|en|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|WD=Q6383831|lt=Keisei-Nishifuna Station|1=Keisei-Nishifuna Station}}

{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]

}}
[[Katsushika Shrine]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Katsushika Shrine''' (葛飾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|WD=Q271417|lt=Funabashi|1=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan.

== Enshrined Deities ==  
The shrine is dedicated to:  
* [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] (瓊瓊杵尊)  
* [[Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto]] (彦火火出見尊)  
* {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}} (熊野大神)

== History ==  
The name "Katsushika" is commonly found in place names across Japan and refers to fertile, beautiful land covered in vines ("kudzu"). The historical text ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions the [[Katsushika District]] and the Kurihara area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.funabashi.chiba.jp/k-seibu/HTML/nakayama/ogurihara002.htm|title=The History of Kurihara|publisher=Funabashi City|accessdate=2009-04-13}}</ref><ref>[https://adeac.jp/funabashi-digital-museum/top/ Funabashi Digital Museum - Kurihara]</ref>  

The shrine originally stood near Katsumata Pond (now [[Katsumata Park]]) under the name "Kumano Shrine," which enshrined {{ill|Kumano Okami|ja|熊野大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Okami|qq=}}. Nearby, a smaller shrine known as "Katsushika Jinja Motomiya" (Katsushika Great Deity) was located near what is now Katsushika Junior High School. A nearby spring called "Kazura no I" (葛羅の井) served as a sacred water source for the shrine.  

The shrine's torii from the ''Bunka'' era (1804–1818) bears an inscription stating that it was the "Sōja" (general shrine) for Katsushika District. This legacy was inherited by the current Katsushika Shrine, which was officially established in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 5 (1916) when the smaller shrine was merged with Kumano Shrine. The combined shrine was renamed "Katsushika Shrine" and elevated to the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社).

== Grounds ==  
Though the shrine grounds are modest at 24 tsubo, it is surrounded by several notable {{ill|black pine|en|black pine|WD=Q4921461|lt=black pine|1=Black pine}} (クロマツ) trees, designated as one of the "Ten Famous Trees of Funabashi." The shrine is located along [[:en:National Route 14|National Route 14]], a route historically known as the [[Narita Highway]], frequented by pilgrims to [[Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] and travelers along the [[Sakura Highway]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.midori-kikin.or.jp/tree/main4.html|title=Katsushika Shrine Black Pine Trees|publisher=Funabashi City Green Fund|accessdate=2009-04-12}}</ref>

== Access ==  
The shrine is accessible from:  
* {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metro|en|Tokyo Metro|WD=Q682894|lt=Tokyo Metro|1=Tokyo Metro}}, or [[:en:Tōyō Rapid Railway|Tōyō Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Funabashi Station|en|Nishi-Funabashi Station|WD=Q128803|lt=Nishi-Funabashi Station|1=Nishi-Funabashi Station}}  
* {{ill|Keisei Electric Railway|en|Keisei Electric Railway|WD=Q83538|lt=Keisei Electric Railway|1=Keisei Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|en|Keisei-Nishifuna Station|WD=Q6383831|lt=Keisei-Nishifuna Station|1=Keisei-Nishifuna Station}}
